Title: Plant-like biosynthesis of isoquinoline alkaloids in Aspergillus fumigatus

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. fsqE is an fsqA-regulated gene within the fsq cluster annotated as an ABC transporter. 2. Authors were unable to delete fsqE, suggesting a possible protective function, but no direct role in fumisoquin production was experimentally established.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Cluster expression analysis; unsuccessful deletion attempts.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed transporter within the fumisoquin cluster; direct metabolic effect not established.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Growth in glucose minimal medium at 37°C; fsqA overexpression background used for pathway activation.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: gpdA promoter, constitutive, for fsqA overexpression
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be relevant for pathway self-protection/export in heterologous production systems, but function requires validation.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Northern blot expression analysis; comparative metabolomics context
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not directly validated as core causal gene for metabolite formation; included because it is a primary fsq cluster gene discussed in results.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: fumisoquin A; fumisoquin B; fumisoquin C
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: fsqE is co-regulated within the fsq cluster by FsqA in A. fumigatus.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Isoquinoline alkaloid specialized metabolites; specific biological function not established in this paper.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: fsqE is regulated by FsqA in A. fumigatus.

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: FAD-dependent oxidase / berberine bridge enzyme-like oxidase
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for fumisoquin isoquinoline alkaloid biosynthesis; deletion abolishes fumisoquin production and causes accumulation of benzyl pyrrole intermediates/shunt metabolites.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Deletion of fsqB in the OE::fsqA background completely abolished production of fumisoquins 1–3. 2. fsqB deletion caused accumulation of benzyl pyrroles 7–8, supporting its role in downstream oxidative cyclization. 3. Recombinant FsqB directly converted N-methyl-DOPA to isoquinoline 12 with near-quantitative conversion and complete regioselectivity. 4. FsqB contains covalently bound FAD and produces hydrogen peroxide during turnover, supporting a BBE-like oxidative mechanism. 5. Dimedone trapping supported formation of an imine intermediate, indicating a two-step mechanism analogous to plant berberine bridge enzyme.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Cluster expression is activated in OE::fsqA strains; fsqB is within the fsqA-regulated cluster.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes FAD-dependent oxidative cyclization of an N-methyl catechol-containing precursor to form the isoquinoline ring system.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Isoquinoline alkaloid specialized metabolites; specific biological function not established in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: N-methyl catechol-containing open-chain precursor; experimentally, N-methyl-3,4-dihydroxy-DL-phenylalanine (N-methyl-DOPA, compound 11)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene deletion in OE::fsqA background; comparative metabolomics by DANS and HPLC-MS; recombinant protein expression in E. coli; in vitro enzyme assay; steady-state kinetics; FAD/proteomic analysis; Amplex Red assay; imine trapping assay.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as a functional analog of plant berberine bridge enzyme despite lack of clear sequence homology; requires catechol moiety and secondary β-N-methylamine for activity; FAD is covalently linked to Cys414.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the isoquinoline core of the target fumisoquins.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: fumisoquin A; fumisoquin B; fumisoquin C
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for oxidative cyclization and isoquinoline ring formation; rate-limiting status not established.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Growth in glucose minimal medium at 37°C; fsqA overexpression background used for pathway activation.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: gpdA promoter, constitutive, for fsqA overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: fsqB is co-regulated within the fsq cluster by FsqA in A. fumigatus.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Oxidative cyclization branch of fumisoquin isoquinoline scaffold formation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a fungal biocatalyst for microbial engineering of plant-like isoquinoline alkaloid production due to regioselective BBE-like cyclization activity.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Isoquinoline cyclization product; experimentally compound 12, and in vivo fumisoquin isoquinoline scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative metabolomics (2D NMR DANS; HPLC-MS); proteomics for FAD linkage analysis
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Flavin-dependent amine oxidase / fructosyl amino acid oxidase-like protein

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for fumisoquin biosynthesis; deletion abolishes fumisoquin production and leads to benzyl pyrrole accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Deletion of fsqC in OE::fsqA completely abolished isoquinoline alkaloid production. 2. fsqC mutants accumulated benzyl pyrroles 7–8, indicating blockage before productive isoquinoline formation. 3. Stable-isotope labeling showed the methionine methyl group is incorporated into the fumisoquin ring system at the predicted position, supporting FsqC as the N-methyltransferase.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Cluster expression is activated in OE::fsqA strains; fsqC is within the fsqA-regulated cluster.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group from SAM to the nitrogen atom of the catechol-containing precursor prior to oxidative cyclization.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Isoquinoline alkaloid specialized metabolites; specific biological function not established in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Open-chain fumisoquin precursor prior to oxidative cyclization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene deletion in OE::fsqA background; comparative metabolomics by DANS and HPLC-MS; stable-isotope labeling with methionine.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Predicted fungal functional analog of plant N-methyltransferases in isoquinoline alkaloid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms a required modified precursor for FsqB-catalyzed isoquinoline formation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: fumisoquin A; fumisoquin B; fumisoquin C
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Required pathway step; key control status not established.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Growth in glucose minimal medium at 37°C; fsqA overexpression background used for pathway activation.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: gpdA promoter, constitutive, for fsqA overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream tailoring step before oxidative cyclization
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: fsqC is co-regulated within the fsq cluster by FsqA in A. fumigatus.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: N-methylation branch of fumisoquin scaffold assembly
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be incorporated into heterologous pathway reconstruction for microbial production of N-methylated isoquinoline alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative metabolomics (2D NMR DANS; HPLC-MS); stable-isotope labeling

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for fumisoquin biosynthesis; deletion abolishes fumisoquin production and causes accumulation of non-catechol benzyl pyrroles.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Deletion of fsqG in OE::fsqA completely abolished isoquinoline alkaloid production. 2. fsqG mutants accumulated benzyl pyrroles 7–8, supporting its role in catechol formation needed for cyclization. 3. Pathway logic and mutant metabolite structures support FsqG as the phenol 2-monooxygenase introducing the second hydroxyl group required by FsqB.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Cluster expression is activated in OE::fsqA strains; fsqG is within the fsqA-regulated cluster.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates a phenolic aromatic precursor to generate a catechol moiety required for downstream FsqB-catalyzed oxidative cyclization.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Isoquinoline alkaloid specialized metabolites; specific biological function not established in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Phenolic fumisoquin pathway intermediate lacking the second hydroxyl group
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene deletion in OE::fsqA background; comparative metabolomics by DANS and HPLC-MS; structural inference from accumulated intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Predicted fungal functional analog of plant phenol-hydroxylating steps; catechol formation is necessary because recombinant FsqB requires catechol-containing substrate.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Generates a required precursor feature for direct isoquinoline ring formation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: fumisoquin A; fumisoquin B; fumisoquin C
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Required pathway step; key control status not established.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Growth in glucose minimal medium at 37°C; fsqA overexpression background used for pathway activation.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: gpdA promoter, constitutive, for fsqA overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: fsqG is co-regulated within the fsq cluster by FsqA in A. fumigatus.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Aromatic hydroxylation branch of fumisoquin scaffold assembly
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important for heterologous reconstitution because catechol formation is essential for productive oxidative cyclization.

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: NRPS-like enzyme with adenylation, thiolation, reductase, and PLP-dependent aminotransferase domains
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for early carbon-carbon bond formation in fumisoquin biosynthesis; deletion abolishes fumisoquins and pyrroles and yields shunt isoquinoline 18.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FsqF is an incomplete NRPS-like enzyme lacking a condensation domain. 2. Stable-isotope labeling showed tyrosine and serine, but not cysteine, are incorporated into fumisoquins and pyrroles, supporting FsqF involvement in coupling tyrosine- and serine-derived units. 3. Deletion of fsqF abolished fumisoquins 1–3 and pyrroles 7–8 and led to accumulation of shunt metabolite 18. 4. These data indicate FsqF is required for addition of a serine-derived dehydroalanine equivalent to activated tyrosine, enabling carbon-carbon bond formation. 5. Recombinant adenylation domain showed no activity with standard amino acids in ATP-PPi exchange assays, suggesting unusual substrate handling or incomplete activity in vitro.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: fsqF expression is regulated by FsqA; pathway active in OE::fsqA strains.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely participates in formation/tethering of a serine-derived dehydroalanine equivalent and its carbon-carbon coupling with activated tyrosine-derived precursor.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Isoquinoline alkaloid specialized metabolites; specific biological function not established in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Likely serine-derived dehydroalanine equivalent and activated tyrosine-derived precursor
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene deletion in OE::fsqA background; stable-isotope labeling with tyrosine/serine/cysteine/methionine; comparative metabolomics; recombinant adenylation domain ATP-[32P]PPi exchange assay.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although bioinformatically NRPS-like, it lacks a condensation domain; authors infer FsqF is not responsible for tyrosine activation and instead FsqD likely activates tyrosine. FsqF likely contributes a novel fungal carbon-carbon bond-forming strategy.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies the core carbon skeleton precursor for fumisoquin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: fumisoquin A; fumisoquin B; fumisoquin C
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Essential early step for precursor assembly; key control status not established.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Growth in glucose minimal medium at 37°C; fsqA overexpression background used for pathway activation; isotope feeding experiments.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: gpdA promoter, constitutive, for fsqA overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: fsqF is co-regulated within the fsq cluster by FsqA in A. fumigatus.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early amino acid-derived carbon skeleton assembly module
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important for reconstructing fungal plant-like isoquinoline pathways and for discovering novel NRPS-like carbon-carbon bond-forming strategies.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Serine-derived dehydroalanine-incorporated precursor for downstream fumisoquin formation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative metabolomics (2D NMR DANS; HPLC-MS); stable-isotope labeling
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NRPS-like enzyme with adenylation; thiolation; reductase; PLP-dependent aminotransferase domains

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed to activate tyrosine (and possibly phenylalanine) for condensation in fumisoquin biosynthesis, but direct genetic validation was not obtained because deletion was unsuccessful.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. fsqD is part of the fsq cluster and is regulated by FsqA. 2. The fsqF deletion phenotype and shunt metabolite analysis led the authors to propose that FsqD activates tyrosine for condensation with serine-derived dehydroalanine. 3. Authors could not delete fsqD, so its role remains inferred rather than directly proven. 4. Sequence homology links FsqD to ATP-grasp carboxylate-amine ligases that form aminoacyl phosphates.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Cluster expression is activated in OE::fsqA strains; fsqD is within the fsqA-regulated cluster.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Proposed to activate tyrosine or phenylalanine as an aminoacyl phosphate for subsequent carbon-carbon bond formation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Isoquinoline alkaloid specialized metabolites; specific biological function not established in this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathway inference from mutant metabolite analysis and sequence homology; no direct knockout validation achieved.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unable to delete fsqD, possibly due to protective function; role is proposed, not directly demonstrated.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies activated precursor pool for core fumisoquin carbon skeleton assembly.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: fumisoquin A; fumisoquin B; fumisoquin C
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Growth in glucose minimal medium at 37°C; fsqA overexpression background used for pathway activation.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: gpdA promoter, constitutive, for fsqA overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: fsqD is co-regulated within the fsq cluster by FsqA in A. fumigatus.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potentially important for pathway reconstitution, but direct function requires confirmation.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: fumisoquin A; fumisoquin B; fumisoquin C
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: fsqA regulates expression of fsqF and five adjacent genes in the fsq cluster in A. fumigatus.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Effective pathway activator for unlocking silent/orphan biosynthetic gene clusters and increasing fungal alkaloid production in engineering contexts.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Isoquinoline alkaloid specialized metabolites; specific biological function not established in this paper.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Constitutive overexpression using gpdA promoter activated the cluster; growth in glucose minimal medium at 37°C.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Zn(II)2Cys6-type transcription factor unique to fungi; overexpression caused accumulation of brown pigment in surrounding medium and enabled discovery of fumisoquins.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: fsqF; fsqE; fsqB; fsqC; fsqG; fsqD
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpression of fsqA strongly activates the fsq cluster in A. fumigatus.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates the fsq biosynthetic cluster and promotes production of fumisoquin isoquinoline alkaloids and associated brown/purple pigments.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Cluster-specific essential activator enabling expression of the core fumisoquin biosynthetic genes.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates fsqF and five adjacent cluster genes including fsqE, fsqB, fsqC, fsqG, and fsqD.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression and deletion strains; Northern analysis/RNA blot; comparative metabolomics by DANS and HPLC-MS.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Biosynthesis of fumisoquin isoquinoline alkaloids via transcriptional activation of the fsq cluster
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: gpdA promoter, constitutive, for fsqA overexpression
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FsqA is a Zn(II)2Cys6-type transcriptional activator controlling the fsq biosynthetic gene cluster. 2. Overexpression of fsqA caused accumulation of brown/purple secreted metabolites and enabled production of fumisoquins. 3. Deletion of fsqA abolished cluster-associated metabolites. 4. FsqA regulates expression of fsqF and adjacent genes fsqE, fsqB, fsqC, fsqG, and fsqD, defining fsq cluster boundaries.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Comparative metabolomics (2D NMR DANS; HPLC-MS); Northern blot expression analysis
